channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fetterman was elected to challenge convention. Now, he's challenging his fellow Democrats
item.description: When Democrat John Fetterman got elected to the U.S. Senate from Pennsylvania, many backers had hoped he’d challenge the status quo.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chinese President Xi Jinping will send a special representative to Trump's inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chinese President Xi Jinping will not attend President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration, but he is sending Vice President Han Zheng as his special representative.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gavel in or gavel out? DeSantis calls for amendment reform
item.description: There are still a lot of questions about Governor Ron DeSantis' call for a special session in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas judge says states can revive challenge to abortion pill access nationwide
item.description: A federal judge in Texas is allowing three other states to pursue a challenge seeking to restrict access to the abortion pill mifepristone nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: State senator pushed to the ground, arrested while trying to enter Georgia House chamber
item.description: A Georgia state senator and hardline supporter of President-elect Trump appeared to be pushed to the floor before being arrested while trying to enter the state House chamber on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2025 Legislature is off to a bumpy start
item.description: DFLers made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo earlier this week as both parties fought over control. Republicans pressed forward anyway. Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about the unconventional start to the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: FBI closed DEI office in December, agency says
item.description: The FBI confirmed to Fox News Digital that the agency closed its office of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in December.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom calls for looting to be a felony in evacuation zones amid LA inferno
item.description: After facing pressure from Southern California prosecutors, Gov. Gavin Newsom calls for looting to be a felony in fire evacuation zones as the blazes are not yet contained.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drug used in federal executions under Trump may cause 'unnecessary pain and suffering,' Garland says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is rescinding its protocol for federal executions that allowed for single-drug lethal injections with pentobarbital, after a government review raised concerns about the potential for “unnecessary pain and suffering.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Get ready for an even bigger chill. Siberian air to make Trump swearing-in coldest in 40 years
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The vast majority of Americans are about to get an extended taste of frigid Siberian weather. Another polar vortex disruption will stretch Arctic air across the top of the globe and make Donald Trump's second inauguration the coldest in 40 years, meteorologists said.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Ashley Moody? Meet the Senate's newest member from Florida
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis is sending Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody from Tallahassee to Washington, D.C., to fill Sen. Marco Rubio’s absence in the U.S. Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani’s Florida condo, World Series rings on the line in latest trial
item.description: Rudy Giuliani will again square off in court against two Georgia election workers Thursday, this time seeking to keep his Florida condominium and other prized possessions from the women who won a $146 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Scott Stringer announces run for New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former New York City Comptroller Scott Stringer is joining the 2025 race for New York City mayor, he announced on PIX11 News on Thursday. He has a long political career, previously serving as the Manhattan borough president and a New York State Assembly member.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: How would RFK Jr. handle bird flu? His record on vaccines has experts on edge
item.description: Trump's pick for health secretary has a record of attacking vaccines. In fact he's even taken direct aim at bird flu shots. Some scientists fear this doesn't bode well in case of a crisis.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis hopes Trump will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: "There's been no issue that has been higher on the radar," DeSantis added. "We need all hands on deck.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: NYC mayor: More beds for mentally ill people, more cops on subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- With more NYPD officers heading into the subway system during the overnight hours, New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he is also looking to get mentally ill homeless people off the trains with a new option.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Flags ordered to fly at full-staff in multiple states on Inauguration Day
item.description: The 30-day mourning period, reserved for the death of a sitting or former president, would have overlapped with the inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Biden both claim credit for Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: President Joe Biden and President-elect Donald Trump are both claiming credit for Israel and Hamas agreeing to a ceasefire deal in Gaza after the White House brought Trump’s Middle East envoy into negotiations that have dragged on for months.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Just 2 in 10 confident Trump DOJ will act fairly: Survey
item.description: GOP voters are divided when it comes to their faith in the DOJ being neutral even with the incoming Republican administration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis hopes incoming Trump administration will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis will be speaking in Winter Haven on Wednesday morning at the Polk County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip the inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth beats back blows from Democrats in blistering hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's choice for Defense secretary, on Tuesday emerged largely unscathed from an at times blistering confirmation hearing before the Senate Armed Services Committee.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: NY plan would make community college free: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to make community college free for adults pursuing in-demand careers.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota House DFLers boycott opening session; Republicans elect their own speaker
item.description: DFLers on Tuesday made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo as the parties fight over control. House Republicans pushed ahead anyway in the half-empty chamber. It’s all likely headed to the courts.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. faces a complicated confirmation with some opposition on both sides of the aisle
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s views on vaccines and abortion rights are raising alarms across the political spectrum, as he's one of the more controversial picks for the president-elect's Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: Special counsel in Hunter Biden case denounces president for criticism of the probe
item.description: In a final report on his investigation, special counsel David Weiss said President Biden's criticism "undermines the very foundation of what makes America's justice system fair and equitable.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Las Vegas 'Preview Mania 2025' set for Thursday with a nod to WWE
item.description: About 1,500 business leaders are expected to attend Las Vegas Preview on Thursday at the Las Vegas Convention Center to hear about the trends that are shaping economic growth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota Senate District 60 special election
item.description: Minnesota Senate District 60 is holding a special election to fill the vacancy left by DFL Sen. Kari Dziedzic who died last month.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto to reintroduce bill to fund fight against wildfires
item.description: In the wake of the devastating California wildfires, Nevada Democratic Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto plans to reintroduce a proposal in Congress to better fund fire departments, interagency coordination and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Appeals court dismisses ousted Hillsborough State Attorney's lawsuit against DeSantis
item.description: A federal appeals court dismissed a lawsuit brought against Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis by Andrew Warren, a former state attorney he suspended from his position.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
